claims.summary: Cautilus keeps agent and workflow behavior honest while prompts keep changing.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` keeps agent and workflow behavior honest while prompts keep changing.

unresolvedQuestions: Is the headline promise proven through a representative scenario set, or is it an umbrella over the per-surface claims below?

claims.summary: Users define a protected behavior once and verify it survives prompt, skill, and wrapper changes.

excerpts.verbatim: It is a repo-local contract layer for agent and workflow behavior evaluation: define the behavior you are trying to protect once, then verify it survives prompt, skill, and wrapper changes.

claims.summary: The product's three connected jobs are claim discovery, bounded-packet verification, and budgeted improvement.

excerpts.verbatim: discover declared behavior claims worth proving from selected source docs, verify curated claims through bounded evaluation packets, and improve behavior with budgeted improvement once the proof surface is honest.

claims.summary: Cautilus ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent that a host repo installs without copying a scaffold.

excerpts.verbatim: Ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent, which a host repo can install without copying another scaffold first.

claims.summary: Commands emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume.

excerpts.verbatim: Commands should emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator.
Agents are first-class users of the product surface.

claims.summary: The binary is machine-level and shared across repos while the agent-facing surface stays repo-local and repo-owned.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.
They stay checked into each host repo so evaluation behavior remains reproducible, reviewable, and owned by the repo that declares it.

claims.summary: Host repos can use evaluate fixture, evaluate observation, and post-run evaluate skill-experiment in the current external-adoption slice.

excerpts.verbatim: Host repos can use `cautilus evaluate fixture`, `cautilus evaluate observation`, and post-run `cautilus evaluate skill-experiment` with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and the current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.

claims.summary: skill-experiment compare only compares host-preserved outputs and never clones, installs, or executes skills.

excerpts.verbatim: `skill-experiment compare` compares host-preserved baseline and variant outputs; it does not clone, install, or execute skills.

claims.summary: Claim discovery automation, improve automation, live app-runner workflows, and review-learning capture are opt-in slices until the rewrite closes.

excerpts.verbatim: Treat claim discovery automation, improve automation, live `eval` app-runner workflows, and review-learning packet capture or selected-packet summaries as opt-in product slices until the rewrite closes.

claims.summary: An agent can drive setup by repeating doctor --next-action until doctor reports readiness plus a first_bounded_run.

excerpts.verbatim: If you want to hand setup to an agent, ask it to repeat `cautilus doctor --repo-root . --next-action`, do exactly what the packet says, and stop only after `cautilus doctor --repo-root .` reports readiness plus a `first_bounded_run`.

claims.summary: The spec apex is the specdown entry and the curated claim source of truth lives in the user and maintainer spec indexes.

excerpts.verbatim: [docs/specs/index.spec.md](./docs/specs/index.spec.md) is the top-level "proven on itself" apex and the specdown entry; the user and maintainer spec indexes it links to remain the curated claim source of truth.

claims.summary: Raw discover claims packets are high-recall proof-planning input, not the primary user review document.

excerpts.verbatim: Raw `discover claims` packets remain the high-recall, source-ref-backed proof-planning input, not the primary document a user should review.

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates the discovery packet: reduces false positives, raises likely missing promises, and separates discovery bugs from narrative gaps.

excerpts.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent curates that packet against the repo: reduce false positives, raise likely missing public promises, and separate in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.
The Cautilus Agent curates false positives, likely missing promises, scan boundaries, and review budgets before any eval plan is trusted.

claims.summary: Host repos do not need the website renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.

excerpts.verbatim: The public website report is generated from the claim spec tree, but host repos do not need that renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.

claims.summary: Each claim page pairs a bounded promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.

excerpts.verbatim: Each claim page pairs a bounded product promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.

claims.summary: One checked-in fixture plus a host-owned adapter runner verifies a bounded behavior fixture and produces reopenable observed and summary packets.

excerpts.verbatim: This loop verifies a bounded behavior fixture and produces reopenable observed and summary packets.
You need one checked-in `cautilus.evaluation_input.v1` fixture and a host-owned adapter runner.

claims.summary: Fixture runs become durable eval packets another agent or maintainer can reopen.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` turns the fixture run into durable eval packets that another agent or maintainer can reopen.

claims.summary: An eval summary is evidence for the chosen fixture and adapter path, not a global product verdict.

excerpts.verbatim: The summary is not a global product verdict; it is evidence for the behavior fixture and adapter path that the host repo chose.

claims.summary: Claim discovery turns adapter-owned entry docs and linked Markdown into proof-plan candidates and is planning, not a correctness verdict.

excerpts.verbatim: Claim discovery turns adapter-owned entry docs and linked Markdown into `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1` candidates.
It is proof planning, not a verdict that the repo is correct.

claims.summary: Evaluation exposes two top-level surfaces: dev for AI-assisted development work and app for AI-powered product behavior.

excerpts.verbatim: Evaluation uses two top-level surfaces: `dev` for AI-assisted development work such as repo contracts, tools, and skills, and `app` for AI-powered product behavior such as chat, prompt, and service responses.

claims.summary: Protected scenarios are kept out of tuning so the validation signal stays honest.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` treats the context-recovery case as a protected scenario kept out of tuning so the signal stays honest.

claims.summary: Evidence is stored in a durable file the next maintainer can reopen from disk.

excerpts.verbatim: It stores the evidence in a durable file the next maintainer can reopen from disk.

claims.summary: Packets, CLI commands, and repo instructions are agent-facing interfaces first; HTML is a mirror, not the source of truth.

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike a dashboard-first review tool, `Cautilus` treats packets, CLI commands, and repo instructions as agent-facing interfaces first; HTML is a human-readable mirror, not the source of truth.

claims.summary: Cautilus treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract instead of freezing one prompt string.

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike a prompt manager, `Cautilus` does not freeze one prompt string as the contract — it treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract (`intent-first`).

claims.summary: Iteration is separated from protected validation and evidence stays reopenable from files.

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike a benchmark scrapbook, `Cautilus` separates iteration from protected validation and keeps evidence reopenable from files (`held-out honesty`, `packet-first`).

claims.summary: Adapters, reports, review files, and compare artifacts are first-class product boundaries.

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike ad hoc eval scripts, `Cautilus` makes adapters, reports, review files, and compare artifacts first-class product boundaries (`structured review`).

claims.summary: Search and revision stay explicitly bounded by budgets, checkpoints, and blocked-readiness conditions.

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike open-ended improver loops, `Cautilus` keeps search and revision explicitly bounded by budgets, checkpoints, and blocked-readiness conditions (`bounded autonomy`).

claims.summary: Proof layers are deliberately split: deterministic claims live in ordinary tests and CI while evaluator-dependent behavior goes through cautilus evaluate.

excerpts.verbatim: The proof layers are deliberately split because humans, code, and AI are good at different work.
Deterministic claims belong in ordinary tests and CI.
Evaluator-dependent behavior goes through `cautilus evaluate`.

claims.summary: Cautilus ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` also ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver: multi-generation reflective mutation, protected reevaluation, frontier-promotion review reuse, checkpoint feedback reinjection, bounded merge synthesis, and Pareto-style frontier selection.

claims.summary: CLI and Cautilus Agent entry points share one host-owned adapter and return the same durable decision surface.

excerpts.verbatim: Two entry points share one host-owned `cautilus-adapter.yaml` and return the same durable decision surface.
Claude and Codex use the repo-local Cautilus Agent that `cautilus init` installs under `.agents/skills/cautilus-agent/`.

claims.summary: Results are machine-readable packets plus readable views that another maintainer or agent can reopen, not just a pass/fail bit.

excerpts.verbatim: The result is not just a pass/fail bit: it is a set of machine-readable packets plus readable views that another maintainer or agent can reopen.
The minimum host-repo shape is an adapter, an installed Cautilus Agent, and run artifacts such as `eval-cases.json`, `eval-observed.json`, and `eval-summary.json`.

claims.summary: doctor --next-action gives the next onboarding step, --scope agent-surface covers discoverability, and doctor reports repo wiring readiness.

excerpts.verbatim: Use `cautilus doctor --next-action` for the next onboarding step, `cautilus doctor --scope agent-surface` for agent-surface discoverability, and `cautilus doctor` for repo wiring readiness.

claims.summary: npm run consumer:onboard:smoke is the shortest end-to-end adoption proof against a fresh consumer.

excerpts.verbatim: From this repo, `npm run consumer:onboard:smoke` is the shortest end-to-end adoption proof against a fresh consumer.

claims.summary: consumer-readiness.md is an evidence appendix rather than the canonical bootstrap guide.

excerpts.verbatim: Dogfood and migration evidence lives in [consumer-readiness.md](./docs/maintainers/consumer-readiness.md), which is an evidence appendix rather than the canonical bootstrap guide.
